52	vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.
53	
54	### `cechAugmented_exact` — sections/sheafification route (P5a resolution input)
55	The augmented Čech complex is a resolution of `F` (ANY `O_X`-module, ANY open cover; qcoh/affineness
56	are needed only by the downstream f_*-acyclicity, not by this exactness). Route: reflect `IsZero(Hᵖ)`
57	through the faithful additive `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (3-line `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`+`map_injective`
58	helper — `reflects_exact_of_faithful` needs `[Abelian]`, avoid); the homology SHEAF = sheafification of
59	the presheaf homology (`homologyIsoSheafify`, built); the presheaf homology is **locally zero on the
60	basis `{V ⊆ some Uᵢ}`** because the restricted cover `{Uₛ∩V}` then has a member `=V`, giving the
61	cover-agnostic prepend-`i_fix` contracting homotopy (`CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`/`combHomotopy_spec`,
62	F-valued objectwise analogue), so its sheafification vanishes via `sheafify_kills_locally_zero` + the
63	`toSheaf∘sheafify ≅ presheafToSheaf∘forget` square (`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`).
64	DEAD ends: stalk functor (no `SheafOfModules.stalk`); the tilde/standard-cover vanishing as the local
65	discharger (wrong cover — that's the 02KG tool); the naive "section complex exact over each affine V"
66	(CIRCULAR = `Ȟᵖ(V,·)`≠0). Analogist: `analogies/tosheaf-reflect.md`.
67	The local discharge's only hard piece (**Sub-brick A**, the per-degree section identification
68	`Γ(V,pushPullObj F Yₚ)≅∏_σ Γ(U_σ∩V,F)`) is decomposed into the new shared
69	`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: ONE new-infra leaf (coproduct→product of module sheaves) + four
70	off-the-shelf pieces + differential via `sectionCech_objD_apply` + Sub-brick-B contractibility via the
71	de-privatized `Dependent` engine (prepend `i_fix`, identity since `V≤coverOpen i`). Analogist:
72	`analogies/subbrickA.md`.
73	
74	### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
75	`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
76	is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
77	already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
78	01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
79	`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
80	`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
81	corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
82	restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
83	free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
84	universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

The strategy frames the consumer as `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)=0` for V a general affine open realized via Form-B Ext in the ambient affine scheme. That framing arises from computing `H^q(f⁻¹V, …)` directly inside X; pushing the affineness through the affine morphism `f|_{U_σ}` first appears to collapse "general affine open" back to "whole affine scheme," which the existing `⊤` case already covers. I cannot rule out that the Form-B realization step still needs an affine-open statement for some plumbing reason, which is precisely why the planner must record the rebuttal rather than proceed silently.
60	- **Severity of the omission**: major.

62	## Must-fix-this-iter

64	- **Route `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp`: CHALLENGE (Q2)** — the strategy reduces termwise acyclicity to a *new* "change-of-scheme Serre vanishing for a general affine open V" generalizing `affine_serre_vanishing`. A fresh reading suggests this generalization is avoidable: via the done `isAffineHom_of_affine_separated`, the relevant preimages `(f|_{U_σ})⁻¹V = U_σ ×_S V` are **affine schemes** (whole-space `⊤` case), not general affine opens, so the existing `⊤`-case `affine_serre_vanishing` (02KG, done) plus the sibling open-immersion-acyclicity lane should suffice — with no new affine-vanishing infrastructure. The planner must either (a) adopt the affine-morphism reduction and drop the general-affine-open generalization, or (b) record in `iter/iter-056/plan.md` a concrete reason the Form-B realization genuinely forces vanishing on an arbitrary affine open V (and confirm that proof reuses the 01EO/02KG `_of_localizationAway` basis mechanism, NOT span-cover descent — which the project has already flagged as circular).

Widen the `Iters left` bands or split each into its own sub-rows so progress is legible.
69	
70	## Overall verdict
71	
72	Route A is the correct, standard, on-merits choice and remains sound: goal-aligned, free of phantom Mathlib prerequisites (the EnoughInjectives connector `HasInjectiveResolutions → EnoughInjectives` is genuinely ~6 LOC and the cited `InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι` is verified present), with no sunk-cost framing and no hidden infrastructure deferral — the Sub-brick A new leaf is concretely planned, not punted. The strategy does **not** defer any construction required by the goal. The one substantive challenge is on the consumer sub-route (Q2): the planner should not build a *general-affine-open* Serre vanishing without first ruling out the cheaper affine-morphism reduction (`isAffineHom_of_affine_separated` + open-immersion acyclicity + the done `⊤`-case `affine_serre_vanishing`), which appears to keep every computed cohomology on a whole affine scheme. On Q3 there is no *new* circularity provided the general-affine-open proof (if pursued at all) routes through the 01EO basis mechanism and avoids span-cover descent, which the project already knows is circular. On Q4 the EnoughInjectives connector is sound. Secondary: table cells have drifted into multi-line prose (compress in place this iter) and the active effort estimates are optimistic against the project's own 01I8/02KG overruns.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Sub-brick A: identifying the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
12	  (blueprint `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` … `lem:cechSection_contractible`)
13	
14	This file is the shared "Sub-brick A" chain that
15	
16	1. identifies the degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p]` with
17	   the coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` in `Over X` (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`);
18	2. decomposes the push-pull object `pushPullObj F Y_p` as a product in `X.Modules`
19	   (`pushPull_sigma_iso`) — the single new-infra leaf;
20	3. identifies the sections of each leg over an open `V` with `Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
21	   (`pushPull_leg_sections`);
22	4. assembles the degreewise section isomorphism `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
23	   (`pushPull_eval_prod_iso`);
24	5. promotes these degreewise isos to a complex isomorphism `D ≅ D'`
25	   (`cechSection_complex_iso`); and
26	6. produces the contracting homotopy `Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0` on the concrete complex whenever
27	   `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` (`cechSection_contractible`).
28	
29	The result is consumed by `CechAugmentedResolution.lean` to close the residual `hSec`.
30

48	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
49	in `Over X`.

51	Route:
52	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
53	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
54	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
55	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
56	
57	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
58	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
59	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
60	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
61	
62	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
63	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
64	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
65

67	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
68	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.

83	
84	/- Planner strategy:
85	Goal: `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))` in `X.Modules`.
86	where `Y_p = (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op (mk p))`.
87	
88	This is THE new-infra leaf. The key observation is that, although the opens `U_σ ⊆ X` OVERLAP
89	inside `X`, they are DISJOINT as components of the coproduct scheme `Y_p = ∐_σ U_σ`.
90	
91	Route:
92	(a) TRANSPORT via `cechBackbone_left_sigma`: by the iso from Stub 1, we may work with the
93	    coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` instead of `Y_p`.
94	
95	(b) BUILD comparison map:
96	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
97	    from the projections `pushPullMap F (ι_σ) : pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
98	    induced by the coproduct inclusions `ι_σ : Over.mk j_σ ⟶ Y_p` (going backwards via
99	    the pushPullFunctor, which is contravariant on `Over X`).
100	
101	(c) CHECK iso via `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`:
102	    The forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf = SheafOfModules.forget ⋙
103	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ...` is faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits

108	    Since the components of `∐_σ U_σ` are disjoint in the coproduct topology:

134	/- Planner strategy:
135	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects,
136	where `j_σ = Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme ⟶ X`.

138	Three off-the-shelf identifications, chained:
139	
140	(1) PUSHFORWARD SECTIONS = PREIMAGE SECTIONS (`pushforward_obj_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:155):
141	    `Γ(V, (j_σ)_* N) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, N)` for any `N : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules`.
142	    Apply to `N = (j_σ)^* F = Scheme.Modules.pullback j_σ |>.obj F`.

144	(2) PULLBACK ALONG OPEN IMMERSION = RESTRICTION (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Sheaf.lean:371):
145	    `(j_σ)^* F ≅ F.restrict j_σ` as `(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules` objects.

149	    `Γ(W, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ W, F)` for any `W` in the source scheme.

273	assuming `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`.

275	This is PURELY COMBINATORIAL — no affine vanishing, no qcoh, no tilde.

277	Route:
278	
279	(A) IDENTIFY THE FAMILY: `U'_σ := coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)`.
280	    `D'` is the alternating coface complex of the cosimplicial object

283	(B) MAXIMUM PROPERTY: Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`, we have
284	    `coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix ⊓ V = V`. Therefore `U'_{i_fix..σ} = U'_σ` for any `σ`
285	    (prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the open). Equivalently, the prepend map

7959	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
7960	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
7961	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
7962	  \to S\). Then:
7963	  \begin{enumerate}
7964	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
7965	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
7966	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
7967	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
7968	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
7969	      \(k \geq 0\),
7970	      \[
7971	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
7972	      \]
7973	  \end{enumerate}
7974	\end{lemma}
7975	
7976	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
7977	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
7978	% the sheaf
7979	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
7980	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
7981	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
7982	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
7983	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
7984	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
7985	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
7986	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
7987	\begin{proof}
7988	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero, lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero, lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib, lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero, lem:pushforward_sections_functor}
7989	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
7990	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
7991	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
7992	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
7993	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
7994	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
7995	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
7996	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
7997	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
7998	
7999	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
8000	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
8001	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
8002	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
8003	
8004	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
8005	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
8006	  sheafification of
8007	  \[
8008	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
8009	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
8010	  \]
8011	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
8012	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
8013	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
8014	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
8015	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
8016	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
8017	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
8018	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
8019	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
8020	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
8021	
8022	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
8023	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
8024	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
8025	  for every \(k\),
8026	  \[
8027	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
8028	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
8029	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
8030	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
8031	  \]
8032	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
8033	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
8034	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
8035	
8036	  \emph{Proof details.} The argument rests on three identifications.
8037	
8038	  \emph{(1) The cohomology-presheaf identification.} The objectwise homology in degree
8039	  \(n\) of the pushed-forward resolution complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\), evaluated at an open
8040	  \(V\), is identified with the absolute cohomology presheaf
8041	  \(V \mapsto H^n(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}) = \operatorname{Ext}^n\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)},
8042	  \mathcal{G}\bigr)\). Concretely: \(\operatorname{Ext}^n(X, Y)\) over an injective resolution of
8043	  the second argument \(Y\) is identified with the \(n\)-th cohomology of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)
8044	  cochain complex \(H^n\bigl(\operatorname{Hom}(X[0], \mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)\), via the composite
8045	  of the Ext-to-cohomology-class equivalence and the cohomology-class-to-homology equivalence of the
8046	  Hom complex (the equivalences of Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib}). Taking \(X = j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)}\) and \(Y =
8047	  \mathcal{G}\), the degree-\(k\) Hom group \(\operatorname{Hom}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)},
8048	  \mathcal{I}^k)\) is identified with \(\Gamma(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{I}^k) = (f_*\mathcal{I}^k)(V)\) by
8049	  the corepresentability \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U \dashv \Gamma(U, -)\) of the absolute-cohomology setup,
8050	  degreewise and naturally; the \(\mathbb{N}\)/\(\mathbb{Z}\) indexing of the two cochain complexes is
8051	  matched by the resolution's degreewise comparison isomorphism. The right-derived side is settled by
8052	  the already-used identification of \(R^n f_*\) with the homology of \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\)
8053	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}); no separate ``right-derived equals Ext'' comparison
8054	  lemma is needed, since both compute the same homology over the same injective resolution and meet at
8055	  corepresentability.
8056	
8057	  \emph{(2) Serre vanishing for a general affine open.} The Serre vanishing of
8058	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} is stated on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). For a general affine
8059	  open \(V\), the preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) (respectively \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) is again affine: \(j\) is
8060	  an affine morphism (the helper that \(U \hookrightarrow X\) is affine for \(U\) affine and \(X\)
8061	  separated), so preimages of affines under \(j\) are affine, and one transports the vanishing along
8062	  the canonical isomorphism \(j^{-1}(V) \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\) using
8063	  the naturality of absolute cohomology under that isomorphism.
8064	
8065	  \emph{(3) Locally zero implies zero sheafification.} The
8066	  passage from ``\(H^k(\cdots) = 0\) on a basis of affine opens'' to ``\(R^k f_* = 0\)'' is the
8067	  module-sheafification analogue of Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} (and of its packaged
8068	  form Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}). It is obtained by transporting the
8069	  abelian-sheaf statement across the natural isomorphism
8070	  \(\operatorname{toSheaf} \circ \operatorname{sheafify} \cong \operatorname{presheafToSheaf} \circ
8071	  \operatorname{forget}\) between module-sheafification followed by the forgetful functor and abelian
8072	  sheafification; the ``locally zero'' hypothesis it consumes is exactly the basis-of-affines vanishing
8073	  supplied by identifications (1) and~(2) above.
8074	\end{proof}
8075	
8076	\begin{lemma}
8077	[The pushforward-sections functor and its additivity]
8078	  \label{lem:pushforward_sections_functor}
8079	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSectionsFunctor,
8080	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive}
8081	  \uses{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}

# Mathlib-analogist directive — change-of-scheme cohomology transport (de-risk the `_acyclic` leaf)

## Mode: api-alignment

## Context (minimal)
Project: formalizing that Čech cohomology computes higher direct images. One active lane,
`OpenImmersionPushforward.lean`, is reducing the open-immersion acyclicity `R^q j_* H = 0`
(`j : U ↪ X` an affine open immersion, `H` quasi-coherent on `U`, `q ≥ 1`) to a residual the prover
reshaped to:
  `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)))).rightDerived q).obj H)`
i.e. `Ext^q(jShriekOU(j⁻¹W), H) = 0` for `j⁻¹W` a general affine open of the affine scheme `U`.

The project already has the ⊤-case Serre vanishing `affine_serre_vanishing` :
`Ext^q(jShriekOU ⊤, F) = 0` over `Spec R` (whole-space), and the helper
`isAffineHom_of_affine_separated` (so `j` is an affine morphism, hence `j⁻¹W` is an affine SCHEME).
The CHOSEN route is to transport the ⊤-case along the canonical scheme isomorphism
`j⁻¹W ≅ Spec Γ(j⁻¹W, 𝒪)` rather than build a general-affine-open cover system. The prover has
already built (axiom-clean) `rightDerivedNatIso` (a natural iso of additive functors transports
their n-th right-derived functors) and `sectionsFunctorCorepIso`
(`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`).

## Structural problem (what I need you to align)
Find Mathlib's idiom — and the exact decl names + imports — for **transporting a right-derived /
Ext cohomology computation across an isomorphism of schemes** `φ : Y ≅ Spec R` (here `Y = j⁻¹W`,
`R = Γ(Y,𝒪)`), in the category of `𝒪_Y`-modules (`SheafOfModules`/`Scheme.Modules`). Concretely:

1. **`isoSpec`**: confirm the exact Lean name + signature of the canonical iso of an affine scheme
   with `Spec` of its global sections. Candidates: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isoSpec`,
   `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffine.isoSpec`. Give the real one and its hypotheses.
2. **Module-category transport**: does a scheme isomorphism `φ : Y ≅ Z` induce an equivalence (or at
   least a compatible functor pair) on `Y.Modules ≌ Z.Modules` in Mathlib? Name it
   (e.g. via `Scheme.Hom.pushforward`/`pullback` of an iso, `SheafOfModules` transport along a
   homeomorphism/iso of sites, or `Scheme.Modules` functoriality). If Mathlib has NO such packaged
   equivalence, say so plainly (gap) and give the cheapest project-side construction shape.
3. **Compatibility with `jShriekOU` and `Ext`**: the cohomology is `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, −)` /
   `(preadditiveCoyoneda (op (jShriekOU V))).rightDerived q`. What is the minimal Mathlib-idiomatic
   way to get `Ext^q` (or `rightDerived coyoneda`) to transport across the module-category
   equivalence so that the ⊤-instantiation `affine_serre_vanishing` over `Spec R` discharges the
   `j⁻¹W` case? Is `rightDerivedNatIso` (already built) the right vehicle, and what natural iso of
   functors feeds it here?

## Specific questions
- Q1: exact name/signature of the affine-scheme ≅ Spec(Γ) iso, and any `IsAffine`/`Γ`-functor
  lemmas (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`, `Scheme.isoSpec_hom`, etc.) needed to identify `Γ(Y,−)` with
  `Γ(Spec R,−)` across it.
- Q2: is there a Mathlib `SheafOfModules`/`Scheme.Modules` equivalence induced by a scheme iso?
  If absent, what is the smallest sound project-side bridge (and is it comparable in size to the
  01I8 sheaf-infra route, which overran badly — flag the risk).
- Q3: the cleanest path from `affine_serre_vanishing` (⊤ over Spec R) to
  `IsZero(Ext^q(jShriekOU(j⁻¹W), H))` — which existing project pieces (`rightDerivedNatIso`,
  `sectionsFunctorCorepIso`, `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`) chain, and in what order.
- Q4 (counter-check): is the transport-along-isoSpec route actually SOUND and cheaper than building
  a `BasisCovSystem U` over arbitrary affine opens of `U`? If you find it secretly forces the same
  general-affine-open machinery (i.e. the transport is circular or needs cohomology functoriality
  Mathlib lacks), say so — that is a critical finding the planner must hear before spending prover
  budget.

## Deliverable
Write `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md` with: the verified Lean names (with `[verified]`/
`[gap]` tags), the recommended chain, the LOC/risk estimate, and an explicit PROCEED / ALIGN-WITH-
MATHLIB / WALL verdict on the transport route vs the cover-system alternative. Also the task_results
report.
EOF

and the refactor `fix-csi-build` (which made `CechSectionIdentification.lean` compile → project build
GREEN). I picked up from those reports and completed the phase.

## Entering state (verified)
- iter-055 ran both lanes PARTIAL + a structural round. Lane 1's shared Sub-brick A file
  `CechSectionIdentification.lean` (6 stubs) shipped RED (namespace/`∏ᶜ` typos) but was fixed this iter
  by refactor `fix-csi-build`; `lake build` GREEN (8335 jobs).
- Inline sorry: 6 (CSI Sub-brick A stubs) + 2 frozen (P5b protected, dead `affine`) + 1 (`hSec`) +
  2 (OpenImmersionPushforward `_acyclic`/`_comp`).

## What I did this phase
1. Processed both iter-055 prover results (CechAugmentedResolution +1 glue; OpenImmersionPushforward +5
   corepresentability helpers, `_acyclic` leaf reshaped geometry-free) → cleared the two prover result files.
2. Read the three already-run iter056 critics + the refactor report.
3. **Dispatched blueprint-writer `openimm-transport` (round 1)** to fix the blueprint-reviewer must-fix
   (the change-of-scheme Serre transport had no Lean anchor) — concurrently with **mathlib-analogist
   `change-of-scheme-cohomology`** to de-risk that transport.
4. **The analogist returned a WALL finding** (see Decision below). **Re-dispatched blueprint-writer
   `openimm-correct` (round 2)** to REPLACE the round-1 (wall) route with the sound two-need split.
5. blueprint-clean `iter056` (purity) → scoped blueprint-reviewer `iter056-recheck` →
   `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` **complete:true / correct:true, 0 must-fix**; HARD GATE CLEARS
   for both `CechSectionIdentification.lean` and `AffineSerreVanishing.lean` (fast path).
6. Updated STRATEGY (compressed the two drifted active-table cells + trimmed "ACTIVE this iter" per the
   strategy-critic format finding; rewrote the consumer row/route to the two-need split; widened estimates
   ~2–3 → ~3–5 per both critics' OVER_BUDGET/optimistic flags). Updated PROGRESS (2 lanes) + this sidecar.

## Decision made

### D1 — Route 2 (open-immersion acyclicity): adopt the analogist's SOUND two-need split; REJECT both the naive isoSpec transport AND the strategy-critic's Q2 avoidance.
- **Chosen:** general-affine-open Serre vanishing via **Need#2** (enlarge `affineCoverSystem` basis B from
  `{D f}` to all affine opens — `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` gives the vanishing with NO restriction
  functor; only `surj_of_vanishing`/`standard_cover_cofinal` need `isCompact_basicOpen→IsAffineOpen.isCompact`,
  ~40–80 LOC) + **Need#1** (whole-scheme `U≅SpecΓU` Ext transport via `Scheme.isoSpec`+`Ext.mapExactFunctor`,
  ~60–120 LOC). This iter's prover lane = Need#2 (cheapest, no new gap, foundational).
- **Why:** mathlib-analogist `change-of-scheme-cohomology` proved the naive route the blueprint had been
  carrying (transport `Ext^q(jShriekOU(j⁻¹V),H)` along the OPEN-SUBSCHEME iso `j⁻¹V≅SpecΓ(j⁻¹V)`) FORCES
  `U.Modules→V.Modules` restriction, i.e. restriction-preserves-injectives — the 200–500 LOC `j_!` gap the
  project deliberately pivoted away from in iter-026 (Form B). `V↪U` is an open immersion, not an iso, so
  there is no `U.Modules≌V.Modules`. The whole-affine `U≅SpecΓU` IS a genuine equivalence but sends the
  proper open `V⊆U` to a general (non-distinguished) affine open of `SpecΓU` — so the ⊤-only
  `affine_serre_vanishing` still doesn't apply; the general-affine-open vanishing is genuinely unavoidable.
  The sound discharge stays AMBIENT (Need#2) — no restriction functor anywhere.
- **LOC/risk:** ~100–200 LOC total, low–medium, NO new Mathlib gap. Reversal signal: if Need#2's
  basis enlargement breaks the existing ⊤-case `affine_serre_vanishing` or `affineCoverSystem` consumers
  (it shouldn't — ⊤ is an affine open, strict generalization), revert to a separate
  `affineCoverSystemGeneral` rather than mutating the existing one.

### Rebuttals (required by the critics' guidance)
- **strategy-critic Q2 (CHALLENGE, "general-affine-open vanishing is avoidable via the affine-morphism
  reduction") — REBUTTED.** The analogist's fresh reading proves it is NOT avoidable: boundary-straddling
  affine opens `W⊆X` force `j⁻¹W` to be a general (non-distinguished) affine open; the affine-morphism
  reduction still lands on a general affine open, not `⊤`. The cheap discharge is not "avoid the
  generalization" but "do the generalization AMBIENT via basis enlargement" (no restriction, no new gap).
  Recorded; strategy-critic's spirit (don't build heavy restriction infra) is honored — we build ~40–80 LOC
  of basis enlargement, not the 200–500 LOC `j_!` route.
- **progress-critic Route 2 (CHURNING; corrective = "blueprint-writer + effort-breaker on the wall
  THIS iter") — addressed by a STRONGER move.** I dispatched blueprint-writer (route correction) +
  mathlib-analogist (which both de-risked AND reframed the wall). I did NOT dispatch effort-breaker: it
  would have decomposed the OLD (wall) framing, which is now deleted. The analogist's reframe collapses the
  "wall" into two bounded no-gap needs; dispatching a prover on the cheapest (Need#2) this iter is the
  concrete corrective. Recorded as the rebuttal to the effort-breaker recommendation specifically.
- **progress-critic Route 1 (CHURNING; corrective = prover EXECUTION on the stubs, min close Stub3+Stub1) —
  adopted verbatim.** Lane 1 dispatches the prover on the now-compiling Sub-brick A stubs; min-success
  Stub3+Stub1 recorded in the objective. No further structural round.

## Subagent log (this phase + the resumed-from prior attempt)
- progress-critic `iter056` (prior) — both routes CHURNING; correctives above.
- strategy-critic `iter056` (prior) — SOUND + Q2 CHALLENGE (rebutted) + format DRIFTED (fixed).
- blueprint-reviewer `iter056` (prior) — chapter complete:partial, 1 must-fix (change-of-scheme transport).
- refactor `fix-csi-build` (prior) — fixed CSI build; project GREEN.
- mathlib-analogist `change-of-scheme-cohomology` (this phase) — **WALL finding**; the pivotal input.
- blueprint-writer `openimm-transport` (round 1) → superseded by `openimm-correct` (round 2).
- blueprint-clean `iter056`; blueprint-reviewer `iter056-recheck` → **gate CLEARS both lanes**.

## Subagent skips
- strategy-auditor / dag-walker / effort-breaker / lean-scaffolder: not needed. The analogist already did
  the deep reference-grounded reduction; effort-breaker would decompose a deleted framing; the two TODO
  targets are small and fully recipe-backed in the blueprint + `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`,
  so a scaffolder adds little over the rich PROGRESS recipe.

   in `Over X`; coproduct distributes over finite fibre products; open-immersion pullback-stability.

   via `toPresheaf` faithful/reflects-iso/preserves-limits + iterated
   `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint`/`Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso`. 01I8-comparable; if multi-iter,
   hand off a precise decomposition.
5. **Stub 4 `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (L209, LOW)** — assemble Stub2 + `evaluationPreservesLimitsOfShape` + Stub3.

   via `sectionCech_objD_apply` (CechAcyclic:1513) + `sectionCechProductEquiv` (CechAcyclic:1438).

  general sibling, or leave as-is and add the sibling). ~40–80 LOC, low risk, no new Mathlib gap.

The plan phase for iter-056 is complete. Summary of what I did:

## Iter-056 plan — key outcome: caught and corrected a hidden WALL on Route 2

**Pivotal finding.** A `mathlib-analogist` audit I dispatched proved the change-of-scheme Serre-vanishing route the blueprint had been carrying — transporting `Ext^q(jShriekOU(j⁻¹V), H)` along the **open-subscheme** iso `j⁻¹V ≅ Spec Γ(j⁻¹V)` — is a dead end: it forces `U.Modules → V.Modules` restriction, i.e. *restriction-preserves-injectives*, the 200–500 LOC `j_!` gap the project deliberately pivoted away from in iter-026. The strategy-critic's Q2 "affine-morphism reduction avoids it" was also refuted (general-affine-open vanishing is genuinely unavoidable).

**Corrected route (sound, no new Mathlib gap):**
- **Need #2** — enlarge `affineCoverSystem`'s basis `B` from `{D f}` to all affine opens; `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` then gives general-affine-open Serre vanishing with **no restriction functor** (only `surj_of_vanishing`/`standard_cover_cofinal` need `isCompact_basicOpen → IsAffineOpen.isCompact`). ~40–80 LOC.
- **Need #1** — whole-scheme `U ≅ Spec Γ(U)` (a genuine equivalence) Ext transport via `Scheme.isoSpec` + `Ext.mapExactFunctor`. ~60–120 LOC.

**Process:** I re-dispatched the blueprint-writer (round 2) to replace the wall route, ran blueprint-clean, and the scoped blueprint-reviewer re-check returned the chapter **complete + correct, 0 must-fix** → hard gate **CLEARS** both lanes (fast path).

**Two prover lanes dispatched (both `mathlib-build`, build green):**
1. `CechSectionIdentification.lean` — fill the 6 Sub-brick A stubs (progress-critic CHURNING corrective = execution; min-success Stub 3 + Stub 1).
2. `AffineSerreVanishing.lean` — scaffold + prove the Need #2 general-affine-open vanishing (cheapest, no-gap, foundational; the OpenImmersionPushforward Need #1 + assembly waits on it).

**Rebuttals recorded** in `iter/iter-056/plan.md`: strategy-critic Q2 (rebutted via the analogist), progress-critic's effort-breaker recommendation (replaced by the stronger analogist reframe). STRATEGY cells compressed (format DRIFTED fix), estimates widened ~2–3 → ~3–5. ARCHON_MEMORY + TO_USER updated with the WALL warning so no future agent re-attempts the open-subscheme transport.
